content: Just as language learners in high English curricula build gradual mastery of writing and critical thinking through repeated structured reflection on their own work, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing the scaffolded reflection pedagogy designed for human learners, rather than relying only on inconsistent ad-hoc self-contradiction prompts. This creates a new framework for iteratively refining LLM outputs that mirrors how humans develop reflective problem-solving skills.
